design.title: Introducing Figma Motion: Your Canvas Now Has a Timeline
design.excerpt: Figma Motion brings animation and timing natively onto the design canvas with keyframe timelines, easing controls, and MP4 export — removing the need to hand off motion work to a separate animation tool.

design.title: Figma's Design Agent: Custom Tools, Context, and Skills
design.excerpt: Figma's upgraded AI design agent now supports Skills (packaged team conventions), MCP Connectors to GitHub, Slack and Notion, and shared prompt history — letting agents act on live context from the tools designers already use.

design.title: Rethinking Figma in an AI World
design.excerpt: At Config 2026, Figma responded to AI pressure by expanding its canvas into code layers, motion, and shaders — the real test is no longer competing with other design tools but proving that a canvas-first workflow can stay relevant in a code-first, agentic era.

design.title: AI didn't replace designers — it promoted them
design.excerpt: AI is rewriting the product designer's role from spec-maker to system architect — those who thrive are the ones orchestrating AI rather than competing with it, becoming 'design directors of one' for entire product surfaces.

design.title: Adobe to acquire Topaz Labs
design.excerpt: Adobe announced a definitive agreement to acquire Topaz Labs — maker of Gigapixel AI, Topaz Photo, and Topaz Video — bringing its AI upscaling and NeuroStream on-device inference technology into Firefly and Creative Cloud.

design.title: KeyShot releases KeyShot 2026.2
design.excerpt: KeyShot 2026.2 adds Apple Silicon GPU rendering support (beta), integrates the new cloud-based KeyShot Cloud rendering service, and introduces Node Groups for the material graph — significantly expanding render options for Mac-based design studios.

design.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs, AI Company Offering Photo & Video Editing
design.excerpt: Adobe is acquiring Topaz Labs and its AI-powered Astra video upscaling and Wonder image enhancement tools, with Neurostream technology enabling large AI models to run locally on consumer devices — a major shift for on-device creative AI workflows.

design.title: Gstarsoft updates design portfolio across CAD and BIM
design.excerpt: Gstarsoft launched an integrated CAD+BIM+AI platform combining 2D/3D CAD, BIM, CAM, cloud collaboration, and industry-specific AI models in one connected environment — addressing design and engineering workflows from drawing conversion to AI-assisted visualization.

music.title: Traxsource to label music as human-made or AI-assisted across platform
music.excerpt: Electronic music download store Traxsource will roll out 'Human-Made' and 'AI-Assisted' labels on July 1, 2026, partnering with AI-detection firms SH Labs and SoundPatrol to classify tracks — while fully AI-generated music will be removed from the catalogue entirely.

music.title: Traxsource to introduce labels to identify both human-made and AI-assisted music
music.excerpt: Traxsource's new framework distinguishes AI-assisted tools — like stem separation, mastering or vocal processing — from music generated wholesale by AI, marking an industry-first for electronic music distribution and shaping how producers must declare their AI tool use.

music.title: Traxsource launches AI transparency labels
music.excerpt: Traxsource becomes one of the first DJ-focused download stores to introduce mandatory disclosure labels for AI-assisted tracks, joining Apple Music, Deezer, Spotify and YouTube in a wave of platform-level AI-transparency requirements that will reshape how producers release music.

music.title: Over 21 million copyrighted songs are circulating among AI developers, watchdog tool launched
music.excerpt: The Atlantic's AI Watchdog published four searchable databases covering over 21 million tracks used to train AI music generators including Suno, Udio and Google's Lyria — including music by Skrillex, Peggy Gou and BICEP — and launched a free tool for any artist to check if their songs are in the data.

music.title: Artists can now check whether their music has been used to train AI
music.excerpt: The Atlantic's new searchable AI Watchdog database reveals that dance music artists including Skrillex, Peggy Gou and BICEP had dozens of tracks scraped without consent, giving the electronic music community an unprecedented tool to audit AI music training datasets.

music.title: AI Training Data 2026: The Atlantic Reveals Whose Music Ended Up in Suno and Udio — Yours Included!
music.excerpt: The largest dataset from nonprofit LAION contains 12.3 million tracks pulled from YouTube — equivalent to 91 years of music — revealing the massive scale on which mainstream AI music tools were trained on copyrighted recordings without artist consent.

music.title: Two Notes Audio Engineering releases Genome Intro, a FREE amp and FX suite
music.excerpt: Two Notes released a free entry-level version of their GENOME 2.0 suite featuring CODEX — an AI amp-playback engine that loads neural amp modelling captures from NAM, AIDA-X and Proteus formats — giving producers and guitarists access to AI-powered tone modeling at no cost.

movies.label: Movies - AI Animation
movies.excerpt: Annecy's MIFA animation market is centering AI tools and private investment as the defining forces reshaping animation financing in 2026, hosting closed-door workshops where studios debate how generative AI changes the economics of character, environment, and background production across feature and episodic pipelines.

movies.label: Movies - AI VFX and Post-Production
movies.excerpt: Adobe has agreed to acquire Topaz Labs — the Emmy-winning AI company behind Video AI's upscaling, denoising, and motion interpolation — with plans to integrate its on-device NeuroStream AI models into Firefly and Creative Cloud, expanding the toolkit from AI generation to AI restoration for filmmakers in a single ecosystem.

movies.label: Movies - AI Animation
movies.excerpt: SIGGRAPH 2026's Computer Animation Festival has been announced with a Google DeepMind production session on artist-driven AI-assisted expressionistic animation and a Best in Show for 'Apart,' a 4-technique animated short — previewing the AI-human workflow debates that will define the July conference in Los Angeles.

movies.label: Movies - Hollywood AI Industry
movies.excerpt: Deadline's investigation reveals that a major AI technology company is used across a large share of Hollywood productions but credited on only about one-fifth of them — drawing a parallel to cosmetic surgery's unspoken culture and raising urgent transparency questions as studios quietly deploy AI for VFX cleanup, dialogue polish, and color grading.

movies.label: Movies - Hollywood AI Industry
movies.excerpt: IndieWire analyzes the fierce fan backlash to A24's $75 million Google DeepMind partnership — examining the studio's argument that co-designing AI tools with DeepMind gives filmmakers a voice in shaping technology, versus an audience that equates any AI adjacency with a betrayal of A24's artistic brand promise.

movies.label: Movies - AI Animation
movies.excerpt: IndieWire's rolling Annecy 2026 roundup captures the festival's AI undercurrent alongside new project announcements, with Autodesk Flow Studio demoing live-action-to-3D-scene conversion on the show floor and studios at MIFA negotiating how generative tools reshape animation co-production deals, indie finance models, and the creative economy.

movies.label: Movies - AI VFX and Post-Production
movies.excerpt: postPerspective examines Adobe's Topaz Labs acquisition from a post-production workflow angle, noting how on-device NeuroStream AI for video upscaling, denoising, and motion interpolation integrates into Premiere and Firefly without cloud dependency — a meaningful change for offline edit suites and secure post facilities.

movies.label: Movies - AI Animation
movies.excerpt: Deadline's five Annecy 2026 talking points places AI at the center of the market's agenda — with MIFA running closed-door AI workflow workshops and studios debating how generative tools are disrupting animation production costs, IP protection strategies, and co-production norms before industry standards can catch up.

movies.label: Movies - AI VFX and Post-Production
movies.excerpt: RedShark News rounds up Adobe's June 2026 Creative Cloud update for video professionals: rebuilt AI rotoscoping in Premiere (Object Matte with four AI selection tools replacing Roto Brush), Reflection Removal for glass-shot cleanup, and an offline-capable on-device Remove Tool — practical AI upgrades to everyday post-production workflows.

movies.label: Movies - Hollywood AI Industry
movies.excerpt: Jurassic World Rebirth and Rogue One director Gareth Edwards calls AI 'bigger than CGI' — describing diffusion model tools as a 'second-unit director who is a billionaire on acid' — and argues the technology's value lies in rapid creative iteration during concepting, not in replacing directors' final production judgment.

games.excerpt: Epic Games released Unreal Engine 5.8 at Unreal Fest Chicago on June 17, introducing an experimental MCP plugin that lets AI models such as Claude and Gemini operate directly inside the editor — spawning actors, configuring lighting, and running automation tests — marking AI as official infrastructure for the game-dev pipeline ahead of UE6.

Epic Games revealed Unreal Engine 6 at State of Unreal 2026, announcing a unified engine that merges UE5 and Unreal Editor for Fortnite, with LLMs and generative AI models — including Claude, Codex, and Gemini — positioned as central to content-creation workflows, aiming to 'greatly reduce tedious work' for developers.

Epic Games says LLMs and generative AI models like Claude and Codex are 'playing a central role' in Unreal Engine 6, letting teams build content faster through level assembly, character setup, code assistance, and crash analysis — all accessible via an experimental Model Context Protocol plugin already shipping in UE5.8.

KRAFTON launched a two-week Ally Duo beta in PUBG on June 17 — a live AI teammate named Ella, powered by NVIDIA ACE and a Mistral-Nemo-Minitron-8B small language model, that listens to voice commands, loots, drives vehicles, and fights autonomously, marking the first production deployment of on-device Co-Playable Characters in a major live-service title.

Epic CEO Tim Sweeney, at Unreal Fest, acknowledged that AI tools in Unreal Engine risk producing low-quality 'AI slop' but argues they act as an accelerant for professional indie and AAA creators — defending UE6's deep Claude and Gemini integration as a net gain for craft and creative output.

GamesRadar covers Tim Sweeney's critique of Valve's Steam AI disclosure requirement, framing it as an obstacle to indie devs using AI in their workflows and highlighting the industry rift between transparency advocates and studios embracing AI-assisted content creation and generative asset pipelines.

Steam Next Fest June 2026 reveals that nearly 1,700 of 8,682 demos — roughly one in five — carry a generative AI content disclosure, a figure that has doubled since last year, showing how mainstream AI-generated game assets have become even for small indie studios.

With 1,715 Steam Next Fest demos disclosing AI-generated content, players are calling on Valve to add cover-art stickers, prominent disclosure placement, and filtering tools — a signal that generative AI assets have crossed the mainstream threshold in indie game development in under two years.

Destructoid covers Sweeney's Unreal Fest comments defending AI in games while blasting Valve's disclosure policy, adding critical context: 52 percent of GDC-surveyed devs now say generative AI harms their industry, sharpening the contrast between big studios embracing AI workflows and the broader developer community's growing unease.

books.excerpt: Tim Ferriss reports his book catalog is on pace to sell 80% fewer print copies in 2026 than in 2022, with nearly all the decline since LLMs like ChatGPT and Claude became widely used — framing AI chatbots as a direct replacement for how-to nonfiction and signaling a structural shift for authors and publishers.

Mark Leslie Lefebvre (Director of Business Development at Draft2Digital) discusses licensing his own AI voice through ElevenLabs for passive royalty income, the value of in-person author experiences as AI floods digital content, and how indie authors are navigating a market where AI-mass-produced ebooks are reshaping reader expectations.

Amazon's new Kindle AI features — 'Story So Far' (spoiler-free chapter catch-up) and 'Ask This Book' (inline passage queries) — are rolling out to Kindle devices and the iOS app, alongside Kindle Scribe's AI-powered notebook search and insights, marking the Kindle platform's first major reader-facing generative AI integration.

The 40th Beijing International Book Fair (June 17–21) featured a new Internet Publishing Joint Pavilion showcasing AI-generated content tools and publishing workflow applications from NetEase, Tencent, ByteDance's Fanqie Novel, and 20 other exhibitors, signaling that AI-driven digital publishing is becoming mainstream infrastructure in China's book industry.

A Cambridge workshop report calls for shared infrastructure to make AI use of scholarly content traceable, measurable, and trustworthy — arguing that as generative AI systems are adopted across research workflows, the publishing industry must preserve verifiability and provenance in the scholarly record.

Google Play Books launches 'Book Insights,' a Gemini-powered in-book AI companion that lets readers ask questions about plot and characters, catch up on previous chapters, and clarify text — raising debate about whether AI chatbots embedded in ebooks enhance or undermine the immersive reading experience.

Jane Friedman's curated weekly roundup covers Wiley's $49M in AI licensing revenue, the Dutch Bookpact.ai consent-based licensing platform, authors suing Anthropic post-settlement, and HarperCollins partnering with Toonstar to adapt books into AI-generated short-form videos — a comprehensive snapshot of the AI×books rights and workflow landscape in late June 2026.
